footerCta.title: Ready to meet?
footerCta.body: Apply for free and we'll be in touch soon!

footer.legalLine: Nine Lives Ikigai, Inc. is a California nonprofit public benefit corporation. EIN: 42-2634127.
footer.taxExemptStatusNote: Nine Lives Ikigai, Inc. is recognized as tax-exempt under IRC Section 501(c)(3), effective April 21, 2026. Contributions are tax-deductible under IRC Section 170.

hero.title: Nine Lives Ikigai
hero.label: Where every cat finds its purpose again.
hero.body: We believe every cat deserves a life of purpose and dignity; whether they've known hardship, homelessness, or simply need a second chance. Rooted in ikigai and the art of kintsugi, we find meaning in the mending.

about.title: Our Belief

quote.text: Life can be started over many times. People live while mending.

about.body: We apply this to every cat that comes through our doors: the feral colony cat, the injured stray, the owner-surrendered companion. Their past does not diminish them. Our work is the gold in the cracks.

services.title: What We Do
services.label: Four paths to a better life

items.title: Community Care
items.description: Humane population management through community trap-neuter-return (TNR) programs; stabilizing colonies, reducing suffering, and coexisting with feral cats.

items.title: Foster Care
items.description: Temporary homes that give cats the safety, socialization, and healing they need before finding a permanent family.

items.title: Adoption
items.description: Thoughtful, lasting placements for stray, rescued, and surrendered cats; matching the right cat to the right home.

items.title: Veterinary Aid
items.description: Medical care, spay/neuter access, and emergency treatment for cats in need, regardless of their origins or circumstances.

adoptTeaser.title: Meet Our Cats
adoptTeaser.label: Looking for a home right now

footerCta.title: Every life has a reason. Help us find theirs.

pageHeader.title: Who We Are
pageHeader.label: The gold in the cracks.
pageHeader.body: Nine Lives Ikigai, Inc. is a California nonprofit public benefit corporation devoted to the humane treatment and welfare of cats in need: through trap-neuter-return, foster care, adoption, veterinary assistance, and public education.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.body: We promote the health, welfare, and humane treatment of cats in need, including through trap-neuter-return (TNR) programs, foster care, adoption services, and veterinary assistance for stray, feral, rescued, and owner-surrendered cats, and we educate the public on responsible cat ownership and humane population management.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.body: Nine Lives Ikigai is a newly formed organization. The IRS determined our 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status effective April 21, 2026. Financial reports and our first required annual information return will be posted here once filed.

footerCta.title: Want to get involved?
footerCta.body: Foster, adopt, or volunteer; every role helps a cat find their way home.

pageHeader.title: Become a Foster
pageHeader.label: A safe place between hardship and home.
pageHeader.body: Fostering gives a cat the safety, socialization, and healing they need before they're ready for a permanent family. We provide the supplies and support; you provide the home.

items.title: Time and Space
items.description: A quiet, secure space in your home for the duration of the foster period, typically a few weeks to a few months.

items.title: Daily Care
items.description: Food, litter, socialization, and basic care. We cover all veterinary costs.

items.title: Updates
items.description: Occasional photos or notes to help us track the cat's progress and match them with the right adopter.

items.title: Supplies
items.description: Food, litter, a carrier, and any equipment specific to the cat's needs.

items.title: Veterinary Care
items.description: All medical care, including emergency treatment, is covered by Nine Lives Ikigai.

items.title: Guidance
items.description: Ongoing support from our team for behavioral questions, socialization tips, and anything else that comes up.

footerCta.title: Ready to foster?
footerCta.body: Tell us about your home and we'll follow up with next steps.

pageHeader.title: Find Your Cat
pageHeader.label: Every cat here is ready for their next chapter.
pageHeader.body: Each listing below is a cat in our care: fostered, loved, and waiting. Take your time. The right match is worth it.

empty.filtered: No cats match your current filters.
empty.default: No cats are available for adoption. Please check back later.

processSteps.title: How It Works
processSteps.label: Four steps to bringing your cat home

steps.title: Apply
steps.description: Complete our adoption application. Tell us about your home, lifestyle, and the kind of cat you're hoping to find.

steps.title: Meet Your Match
steps.description: We'll introduce you to cats whose temperament and needs fit your home. Visits are relaxed and unhurried. We let the cat set the pace.

steps.title: Quick Check-in
steps.description: A brief call or video chat to confirm the setup and answer any last questions before your cat comes home.

steps.title: Settle In
steps.description: Your cat comes home with a starter pack and guidance for the first few weeks. We're available whenever questions come up: before, during, and after the transition.

items.question: What is the adoption fee?
items.answer: Kittens (<12 months): $50, Adult (12+ months): $35, Senior (8+ years): $20

items.question: Are fees different for kittens vs. adult vs. senior cats?
items.answer: Yes. Kittens carry a higher fee; adult cats are mid-range; senior cats (8+ years) are reduced.

items.question: Are fees waived or discounted for special needs cats?
items.answer: Special needs cats may be adopted at a reduced fee. In some cases fees are waived entirely. Check individual profiles for details.

items.question: Do you offer senior-for-senior discounts?
items.answer: Yes. Adopters 65 or older adopting a senior cat qualify for a waived adoption fee.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: We accept card, cash, and online payment at the time of adoption.

items.question: How long does the process take?
items.answer: Most adoptions are completed within one to two weeks from application to welcome home.

items.question: Can I reserve or hold a cat while I apply?
items.answer: We can place a short hold on a cat once your application is under review. Contact us to arrange this.

items.question: Can I adopt same-day?
items.answer: Same-day adoption is not available. The application and check-in steps ensure every placement is the right fit.

items.question: What happens if the cat I want is adopted before I am approved?
items.answer: We'll work with you to find another match. Your application stays active and we'll be in touch as soon as a suitable cat is available.

items.question: How will I be contacted after I apply?
items.answer: By email within 2-3 business days of submission.

items.question: Do I need to be a homeowner?
items.answer: No. Renters are welcome provided they have documented landlord approval for cats.

items.question: What if my landlord approval isn't in writing?
items.answer: Written landlord approval, a signed letter or email, is required before adoption is finalized. Verbal confirmation is not accepted.

items.question: Is there an age requirement for adopters?
items.answer: Adopters must be 18 or older. Nine Lives Ikigai operates in California; all adoptions are subject to California law.

items.question: Do children in the home need to meet the cat first?
items.answer: Children 15 and under must meet the cat before adoption is finalized. We'll arrange this during the meet step.

items.question: Do my existing pets need to be spayed or neutered?
items.answer: Yes. Any cats currently in the household must be spayed or neutered prior to adoption.

items.question: Do you have a non-discrimination policy?
items.answer: Yes. Consistent with our Bylaws, we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, age, disability, marital status, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. This applies to all adoption services and programs.

items.question: Do your cats come vaccinated, microchipped, and spayed/neutered?
items.answer: Yes. All cats are spayed or neutered, up to date on age-appropriate vaccinations, and microchipped before coming home.

items.question: What support do you offer after adoption?
items.answer: We remain available by email for as long as you need us, reach us at support@ninelivesikigai.org. Your starter pack includes care guidance for the first few weeks.

items.question: What does the starter pack include?
items.answer: Food for the first few days, litter, a carrier liner, and your cat's veterinary history and records.

items.question: Can I meet a cat more than once before deciding?
items.answer: Yes. We encourage it for cats that need more time to warm up.

items.question: Do you offer any veterinary assistance after adoption?
items.answer: We run a veterinary assistance program for cats in need. If you're facing unexpected medical costs after adoption, contact us. We may be able to help.

items.question: Do you have cats that are good with dogs?
items.answer: Some of our cats are dog-tested and listed as dog-friendly on their profiles. We'll flag this during matching.

items.question: Do you require indoor-only adoption?
items.answer: Yes. All cats are adopted to indoor-only homes.

items.question: What if the cat isn't getting along with my other pets?
items.answer: Contact us. We'll provide guidance first. If the placement isn't working, we'll help arrange a return and work toward a better match.

items.question: What if it's not the right fit?
items.answer: Per your adoption agreement, we ask that you return the cat to us rather than rehome independently. Finding the right match matters more than any single placement.

items.question: Is there a trial period?
items.answer: There is no formal trial period, but we stay closely in touch during the first two weeks and will help troubleshoot any issues that come up.

items.question: What if my circumstances change after adoption?
items.answer: Contact us. Whether it's a move, allergies, or a change in household, we'll work with you on the best path forward for the cat.

footerCta.title: Not ready to adopt?
footerCta.body: Fostering and donating make every adoption possible.

pageHeader.title: Support Our Work
pageHeader.label: Every gift helps a cat find their way home.
pageHeader.body: Your donation funds spay/neuter surgeries, vaccinations, emergency veterinary care, and the day-to-day cost of fostering cats until they're ready for adoption. No gift is too small.

amounts.title: Make a Gift
amounts.label: Choose an amount to get started
amounts.note: Donations are processed securely through Stripe. You'll receive an emailed receipt for your records after your donation is processed.

donate.disclosure: Nine Lives Ikigai, Inc. is a California nonprofit public benefit corporation (EIN: 42-2634127), recognized as tax-exempt under IRC Section 501(c)(3) effective April 21, 2026. Contributions are tax-deductible under IRC Section 170 to the extent allowed by law; consult your tax advisor regarding the deductibility of your gift.

otherWays.title: Other Ways to Give
otherWays.label: Support that goes beyond a one-time gift

items.title: In-Kind Donations
items.description: We welcome donations of food, litter, carriers, bedding, and similar supplies. Contact us to arrange a drop-off; larger or unusual in-kind gifts (estimated value over $1,000) are reviewed under our gift acceptance policy before we can confirm acceptance.

items.title: Amazon Wishlist
items.description: Shop our wishlist and have supplies shipped directly to our foster network.

footerCta.title: Not ready to give right now?
footerCta.body: Fostering and adopting are just as valuable.

pageHeader.title: Get in Touch
pageHeader.label: We'd love to hear from you.
pageHeader.body: Whether you have a question about adoption, want to report a stray, or are interested in volunteering, send us a message and we'll get back to you within 2-3 business days.

form.successMessage: Thank you for reaching out. We'll get back to you within 2-3 business days.
form.errorMessage: Something went wrong sending your message. Please try again, or email us directly at support@ninelivesikigai.org.
form.privacyNote: By submitting this form, you agree to our

items.label: Location
items.value: Based in California. We operate primarily through our foster network, by appointment only.

items.label: Hours
items.value: Monday - Friday, 9am - 5pm PT. We aim to respond to all messages within 2-3 business days.

items.question: I found a stray cat. What should I do?
items.answer: Select 'Report a Stray' in the form above and include the location and any details about the cat's condition. For emergencies, contact local animal control directly.

items.question: Can I visit in person?
items.answer: We operate through a foster network rather than a physical shelter. Meetings with cats are arranged by appointment during the adoption process.

items.question: How do I volunteer?
items.answer: Select 'Volunteering' in the form above and tell us about your interests and availability. We'll follow up with current opportunities.

footerCta.title: Ready to make a difference?
footerCta.body: Adopt, foster, or donate to help cats find their way home.

pageHeader.title: Privacy Policy
pageHeader.body: Nine Lives Ikigai, Inc. ("we," "us," or "our") respects your privacy. This policy describes what information we collect through ninelivesikigai.org and how we use it.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.body: When you submit our contact form, apply to adopt or foster, or sign up for email updates, we collect the information you provide directly, which may include your name, email address, phone number, and the contents of your message.

sections.title: Third Parties We Share Information With
sections.body: We share limited information with service providers who help us operate the site and our programs. Donations are processed by Stripe, which receives your payment information directly. If you sign up for email updates, our email service provider receives your email address to deliver those messages. These providers are authorized to use your information only to provide services to us and are not permitted to sell it.

sections.title: How We Use Information
sections.body: We use this information to respond to your inquiry, process adoption or foster applications, and, if you opt in, to send you email updates about cats in our care and our programs. We do not sell your personal information.

sections.title: Tracking and Do Not Track Signals
sections.body: This site does not use technology that tracks visitor activity across other websites over time, and does not respond differently to browser "Do Not Track" signals because no such tracking occurs.

sections.title: Your Choices
sections.body: You may unsubscribe from email updates at any time using the link in any email we send. As an organizational practice, we will also work with you to access, correct, or delete personal information we hold about you — contact us at support@ninelivesikigai.org with any request or question.

sections.title: How We Protect Your Information
sections.body: We maintain reasonable security procedures appropriate to the nature of the information we collect, to protect it from unauthorized access, disclosure, or misuse. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
sections.body: If we make a material change to this policy, we will post the revised policy on this page and update the effective date below.

privacy.complianceNote: This policy meets the disclosure requirements of the California Online Privacy Protection Act (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 22575 et seq.).

notFound.title: (404) Page Not Found
notFound.body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist.
